canonical_question: How do I design a reliable webhooks system?
aliases: How to build a webhook delivery system
How to send webhooks reliably at scale
constraints: Always sign webhook payloads with HMAC-SHA256 or ed25519 -- never send unsigned webhooks
Receivers must return 2xx within 5-30 seconds -- process asynchronously via queue
Implement at-least-once delivery with idempotency keys -- exactly-once is not achievable over HTTP
Persist events to durable storage before attempting delivery -- in-memory queues lose data on crash
Use constant-time comparison for signature verification -- timing attacks leak secret bytes

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to consume/receive webhooks from a third-party API (Stripe, GitHub)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Refer to that provider's webhook integration docs

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need guaranteed ordering of all events

inputs_needed.question: How many webhook events per second do you expect to send?
Do events need to be delivered in strict FIFO order?
Are you building the webhook sender (provider) or receiver (consumer)?
